title: adiantum recurvatum
intro: Adiantum recurvatum, also known as the bent maidenhair fern, is a species of delicate fern with intricately curved fronds. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Adiantum recurvatum include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and promoting ease of breathing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion, dried in tea blends, or applied topically in the form of a poultice, although its use and preparation may vary depending on regional practices and traditional knowledge.
